title: Docker | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# Docker

## [Supported versions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/docker/#supported-versions "Supported versions")

The level of support for a language is defined as follows:

- Fully supported: Analysis will complete. All the language features are understood and examined.
- Supported: Most language features are understood and examined but the version includes unsupported features. Analysis might break or provide incomplete results.

Versions 1.0 to 1.6 are supported.

## [Language-specific properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/docker/#language-specific-properties "Language-specific properties")

Discover and update Docker-specific [properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/analysis-parameters/ "properties") in: Project **Administration** > **General Settings** > **Languages** > **Docker**

## [Dockerfiles](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/docker/#Dockerfiles "Dockerfiles")

#### [No NoSonar Support:](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/docker/#no-nosonar-support "No NoSonar Support:")

Trailing comments are not permitted in Dockerfiles. For this reason, our Dockerfile parser does not support NOSONAR comments to suppress issues. Issues and hotspots must be reviewed in the UI.

#### [Missing Uniform Filename Convention:](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/docker/#missing-uniform-filename-convention "Missing Uniform Filename Convention:")

Dockerfiles can have all kinds of names and do not need a file extension. For this reason, it is difficult for the scanner and the analyzer to recognize all Dockerfiles. By default, all files named Dockerfile, Dockerfile.\*, or \*.dockerfile are considered Dockerfiles. If other conventions apply, these can be specified via the scanner property sonar.lang.patterns.docker.
